hero.question: Will the DHS shutdown end after March 31, 2026?
hero.prob_take: The 10-point spike signals money flowing in on YES, likely traders betting Congress gets tired of shutdown theater and funds DHS before the deadline hits. At 69.5%, the market is pricing in competence from a divided Congress, which is generous, but the real tell is that March 31 is only 14 months out and both parties eat political damage from extended shutdowns more than they gain leverage.

daily_take.headline: DHS Shutdown Odds Jump 10 Points. Market Pricing In March Deadline Crunch.
daily_take.deck: The market just repriced DHS shutdown resolution odds to 69.5% after a 10-point rip, signaling growing confidence a deal gets done before the March 31 deadline. Volume spiked to $659K, which means real money moved. This looks like positioning ahead of actual negotiations, not panic selling. The sharp play: 70% is still short of certainty. Watch for congressional chop in the next 48 hours. If the bid stays firm above 65%, you're looking at genuine deal-making momentum.
